facts.title: Wired and fast
facts.fact: South Korea has some of the world's fastest average internet speeds and near-universal smartphone use. Transit, payments, and even restaurant orders are heavily app-based.
facts.note: **와이파이** (wifi) is widely available in cafés, subways, and buses.

facts.title: High-speed rail
facts.fact: The KTX high-speed train links major cities at up to 300 km/h, putting most of the country within a few hours of Seoul.
facts.korean_en: I go to Busan by train.
facts.note: In 기차로, the particle **(으)로** marks the means: "by train."

facts.title: Memory chip leader
facts.fact: South Korea is the world's top maker of **memory semiconductors**, the chips that store data in phones and computers. Samsung and SK hynix together make most of the global supply.
facts.korean_en: Korea makes a lot of semiconductors.

facts.title: Smartphone giant
facts.fact: Samsung is one of the world's largest smartphone makers, and many people in Korea use a Korean-made phone. New models often launch in Seoul first.
facts.note: **쓰다** has several meanings (use, write, wear); here it means "to use."

facts.title: Building the world's ships
facts.fact: Korean shipyards are among the largest on Earth, building giant cargo ships and tankers. The southern port city of Ulsan is a famous shipbuilding center.
facts.note: **배** can mean ship, but also "pear" or "stomach" depending on context.

facts.title: The Nuri rocket
facts.fact: **Nuri (누리호)** is South Korea's home-built space rocket. In 2022 it successfully carried satellites into orbit, making Korea one of the few countries with its own launch vehicle.
facts.korean_en: Korea sends a rocket into space.
facts.note: **누리** is a pure-Korean word meaning "world," and **호** is a suffix for named vehicles.

facts.title: Danuri at the Moon
facts.fact: **Danuri (다누리)** is Korea's first lunar orbiter. Launched in 2022, it travels around the Moon and sends back photos and science data.
facts.note: The name **다누리** blends 달 (moon) with 누리다 (to enjoy): "enjoy the moon."

facts.title: Powering electric cars
facts.fact: Korean companies like LG, Samsung SDI, and SK On are major makers of **EV batteries** used in electric cars worldwide. Batteries are now one of Korea's biggest exports.
facts.note: **전기** (electricity) combines with 자동차 to form 전기차, "electric car."

facts.title: Robots at work
facts.fact: South Korea uses more industrial **robots** per worker than almost any other country. Robots are common in factories, and you may even see them serving food in cafés.
facts.note: The particle **에서** marks where an action happens: 공장에서 = "at the factory."

facts.title: Hyundai and Kia cars
facts.fact: Hyundai and Kia are Korean carmakers sold all over the world. Together they form one of the largest car groups on the planet.
facts.korean_en: This car is made in Korea.
facts.note: **현대 (Hyundai)** means "modern times," and **기아 (Kia)** suggests "rising from Asia."

facts.title: First with 5G
facts.fact: In 2019 South Korea became one of the first countries to launch nationwide **5G** mobile networks. Fast mobile data made video and gaming on phones very popular.
facts.note: **아주** means "very" and goes before adjectives: 아주 빠르다 = "very fast."

facts.title: Early broadband nation
facts.fact: Korea built fast home **broadband** very early, in the late 1990s and 2000s. This helped online gaming, streaming, and internet cafés grow quickly.
facts.korean_en: I use the internet at home.
facts.note: Koreans often say 인터넷을 하다, literally "to do the internet," for going online.

facts.title: Electronics for the world
facts.fact: Electronics such as chips, screens, and phones are among Korea's top exports. Companies like Samsung and LG sell their products in nearly every country.
facts.korean_en: Korea sells a lot of electronics.

facts.title: Screens everywhere
facts.fact: Korean companies lead the world in **OLED display** screens used in TVs and phones. These thin screens show bright colors and deep blacks.
facts.korean_en: The TV screen is very big.
facts.note: **화면** (screen) appears in 큰 화면 "big screen" and 화면을 보다 "to look at the screen."

facts.title: Nuclear power and exports
facts.fact: South Korea gets a large share of its electricity from **nuclear power plants**. It also builds reactors abroad, including a major project in the United Arab Emirates.
facts.korean_en: Korea makes a lot of electricity.
facts.note: **원자력** is "nuclear power"; 원자 means "atom" and 력 means "power."

facts.title: Sun and wind energy
facts.fact: Korea is building more **solar and wind power** to make cleaner electricity. Large offshore wind farms are planned along the southern and western coasts.
facts.note: **햇빛** (sunlight) combines 해 (sun) and 빛 (light), with a linking ㅅ sound.

facts.title: Medicine and biotech
facts.fact: Korea has a growing **biotech** industry that makes medicines and vaccines. Some Korean companies produce drugs for hospitals around the world.

facts.title: PC bang gaming culture
facts.fact: Internet cafés called **PC방 (PC bang)** are popular places to play games together. Cheap, fast computers helped make Korea a center of competitive e-sports.
facts.korean_en: I play games with a friend.
facts.note: **방** means "room"; PC방 is literally a "PC room."
